title: gymnogramma subdigitata
intro: Gymnogramma subdigitata, also known as the finger fern or gymnogramma fern, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gymnogramma subdigitata include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in reducing fever and alleviating pain. Gymnogramma subdigitata can be prepared as a herbal infusion, where the leaves are steeped in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal benefits.
